meaning: n.无数,无数的人或物,<诗>一万adj.无数的,一万的,种种的
examples: There is equilibrium between a whole myriad of reactants and products.
They would have to explain myriad compromises and abdications along the way.
Designs are available in a myriad of colours.
For a drink you can choose from myriad charming bars, enoteche (wine bars) and pubs.
Many travellers come to Tokyo to sample a forward-thinking city resplendent with myriad technical wonders.
